channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Minister investigates Zuma's foreign policy and High Commissioner's role in India meeting
item.description: South Africa's Minister of International Relations and Cooperation, Ronald Lamola, has raised concerns about former President Jacob Zuma's recent visit to India, where he met Ajay Gupta, one of the Gu...

item.title: Key News Updates: Marcoleta's Plunder Case, Duterte Impeachment, and More
item.description: The article highlights several critical news developments in the Philippines. Senator Rodante Marcoleta faces a plunder case over alleged P75-million campaign donations, with the Office of the Ombudsm...

item.title: Former Benue SSG Dies After Bandit Attack
item.description: Professor David Salifu, a former Secretary to the Benue State Government, died after being shot by bandits during a travel incident on the Wukari-Zakibiam road. The attack occurred on Thursday morning...

item.title: Controversy Surrounds Femi Gbajabiamila, Tinubu’s Chief of Staff
item.description: President Bola Tinubu’s chief of staff, Femi Gbajabiamila, faces allegations of facilitating the appointment of Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew as director-general of a non-existent agency, the Presidential F...

item.title: Canadian Country Star Tommy Hunter Dies
item.description: Tommy Hunter, a prominent Canadian country music artist known for his contributions to the genre, has passed away. The news was announced by The Canadian Press on July 3, 2026. Hunter's career spanned...

item.title: Atiku Calls for Independent Probe into PFIPC Scandal
item.description: Former Vice-President Atiku Abubakar has demanded President Bola Tinubu launch an independent investigation into the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC) scandal, accusing the a...

item.title: Google Play Invests $1 Million in African Game Developers to Boost Global Reach
item.description: Google Play has launched its first Indie Games Fund, committing $1 million to support rising game studios across 32 African countries. The initiative provides equity-free capital, technical support, a...

item.title: FIA investigates human placenta smuggling ring in Islamabad
item.description: The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) has raided an illegal processing facility in Islamabad, uncovering 500 kilograms of what is believed to be human placenta, leading to the arrest of five individu...

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo scores first World Cup knockout goal as Portugal defeats Croatia
item.description: The article details a dramatic World Cup 2026 match between Portugal and Croatia, where Cristiano Ronaldo finally secured his first knockout goal for his nation. The video, hosted by Max Rushden and f...

item.title: Trump calls for removal of Fed Governor Lisa Cook amid economic tensions
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump has publicly stated his intention to pursue the removal of Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, a move that has sparked debate among economists and political analysts...

item.title: Kajiado Governor Race: Ole Metito Leads in Latest Poll
item.description: A new opinion poll by Politrack Africa has revealed that State House Comptroller Katoo Ole Metito is leading the race for the Kajiado governorship with 50.9% support, significantly ahead of his rivals...

item.title: Proteas' Disappointing T20 World Cup Campaign: Kapp Shines as Player of the Tournament
item.description: The South African women's cricket team, the Proteas, exited the T20 World Cup in the semifinals after a 40-run defeat to England. Despite the team's overall underwhelming performance, all-rounder Mari...

item.title: Cameroonian President Paul Biya receives medical treatment in Switzerland with family
item.description: Cameroon's President Paul Biya has been undergoing medical treatment in a private clinic in Geneva, Switzerland, since June 7, 2026. The president, accompanied by his wife Chantal, has been joined by ...

item.title: Christian Brothers seek pause on payouts to child sexual abuse survivors citing lack of funds
item.description: The Christian Brothers, a Roman Catholic religious order, have been granted a temporary pause on paying compensation to child sexual abuse survivors due to claimed financial difficulties. This decisio...

item.title: Tanzania's Import Taxes Fuel 35% of Government Revenue
item.description: Tanzania's import taxes generated 35.7% of total government tax revenue between October and April 2026, according to the Bank of Tanzania's report. The seven-month period saw import taxes collect 7.21...

item.title: Man Convicted for 2019 Murder in Chikkamagaluru
item.description: A man accused of murdering Sumithra Bai in 2019 was convicted by a Chikkamagaluru court and sentenced to seven years' rigorous imprisonment along with a ₹50,000 fine. Kumar Naik, the convict, killed B...

item.title: Trump's Obsession with Washington D.C. Renovations Amid Legal Challenges
item.description: President Donald Trump's fixation with transforming Washington, D.C., has intensified since his return to the White House in January 2025, according to a former campaign official. The obsession began ...

item.title: BSP announces expansion of zero transfer fee initiative among Philippine banks
item.description: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) has announced plans to expand its zero transfer fee initiative, encouraging more commercial banks to eliminate charges for domestic fund transfers. This move aims...

item.title: A basketball story helps boys navigate grief, friendship, and hope
item.description: Fiona Harris' new middle-grade novel, The Rebound, uses basketball as a metaphor to help boys process complex emotions like grief, friendship, and hope. The story follows Harry, a boy who loses his pa...

item.title: ADC demands judicial inquiry into PFIPC scandal over institutional corruption
